---
title: "About"
---

## Course Instructor

This course is offered by the **Department of Development Economics**, Kerala Agricultural University, as part of the BSc Hons Cooperation and Banking programme (6th Semester).

---

## About the Course

**ECON 3209 — Econometrics with Python** introduces students to empirical economic analysis using Python. The course integrates theoretical econometrics with hands-on computational practice, preparing students for data-driven research in cooperative banking, agricultural economics, and allied fields.

---

## About Kerala Agricultural University

[Kerala Agricultural University (KAU)](https://kau.in) is the premier agricultural university in Kerala, India. Established in 1971, KAU offers undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programmes in agriculture, horticulture, forestry, fisheries, food science, cooperation and banking, and other allied sciences.

---

## Technology

This course website and all interactive slide decks are built with:

- [Quarto](https://quarto.org) — scientific and technical publishing
- [Pyodide](https://pyodide.org) — Python in the browser (via WebAssembly)
- [quarto-pyodide](https://github.com/coatless/quarto-pyodide) — Quarto extension for live Python cells

All course materials are open and freely accessible.

---

## Acknowledgements

The Python programming sections of this course draw heavily from the freely available notes of:

- **Kevin Sheppard** (University of Oxford) — *Introduction to Python for Econometrics, Statistics and Data Analysis* — an outstanding resource for economists learning Python, available on GitHub at [bashtage/python-introduction](https://github.com/bashtage/python-introduction).
- **Weijie Chen** — *Econometrics with Python* — a comprehensive open-source econometrics course in Python, available on GitHub at [weijie-chen/Econometrics-With-Python](https://github.com/weijie-chen/Econometrics-With-Python).

The econometrics content follows the treatment in **Maddala & Lahiri** (*Introduction to Econometrics*, 4th ed., Wiley) and **Wooldridge** (*Introductory Econometrics*, 7th ed., Cengage). Students are encouraged to consult both texts alongside the course slides.
